query: right so we need a way for admins to bulk-invite users by uploading a csv. there's already a single-invite endpoint in src/api/invites.ts, i'm thinking we reuse most of it but i'm not sure how to handle partial failures
can you add a --dry-run flag to the migrate command? should print what it would do and exit 0 without touching the db
the InvoiceTotals class has grown to about 600 lines and four of us keep hitting merge conflicts in it. i want to break it apart but i don't want to change any behaviour
lets build out the notifications system. email + in-app to start, slack later maybe. no idea how you'd structure it tbh, i've never touched that part of the codebase

we're switching the auth flow from session cookies to short-lived JWTs with a refresh token. this touches the middleware, the login handler, and the mobile client's token store. use committee mode for this one
just quickly add a healthcheck endpoint at /healthz that returns 200 and the git sha. should take you 2 minutes

product wants the export to also include the cancelled orders, currently we filter them out in the query. changing that changes what every existing customer sees in their exports so i want to think about it properly first

I'd like to introduce a caching layer in front of the pricing service. Redis is already in the stack for sessions. Walk me through how the pricing code is currently structured before we decide anything, I only joined last week
need to add rate limiting to the public api. per-key, sliding window, and it has to work across our 3 app servers
